heading: "Il faut imaginer Sisyphe heureux."
lede: One must imagine Sisyphus happy. A small answer to the largest question I keep asking — that the meaning is not at the end, it is in the experiencing: every field not yet seen, every thing not yet attempted, every hour not yet lived.
blurb: One must imagine Sisyphus happy. A small answer to the largest question I keep asking.

heading: "Anxiety is the dizziness of freedom."
blurb: On the season of comparing oneself, the weight of an undecided future, and the slow work of taking the stone back into one's own hands.

heading: "Love consists in this…"
blurb: A late-evening note on the kind of love that lets two solitudes stay separate — and on attention as the form love takes when one chooses to give it.

heading: "No man steps in the same river twice."
blurb: Three years downstream from an exam that was meant to decide everything — on time, the open question, and the mercy of being able to look back.

heading: Games worth bragging about
blurb: Not a review — honest favorites and the moments that made them stick.

heading: How a student put himself on the internet
blurb: Building this site from a blank file — the choices, the lessons, the small joys of vanilla CSS.

heading: On building small tools
blurb: Why one-page utilities still beat the fanciest framework — and what they teach you about taste.
